SEARCH
Package cloudpage.service

Class TrashService

java.lang.Object
cloudpage.service.TrashService
@Service public class TrashService extends Object
Implements a per-user trash (soft delete). Deleting a file moves it into a hidden .trash directory under the user's root and records it in the database, from where it can be listed, restored, or permanently removed. Entries whose retention period has elapsed are purged automatically on a schedule.

The trash directory lives inside the user's root so it stays within the existing path security boundary; it is excluded from normal folder listings.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    TRASH_DIR
    Name of the per-user trash directory, relative to the user's root.

    Method
    Description
    List<TrashEntryDto>
    listTrash(String userId)
    Lists the current contents of a user's trash.
    void
    moveToTrash(String rootPath, String userId, String relativeFilePath)
    Moves a file into the user's trash instead of deleting it permanently.

    Permanently removes a single file from the user's trash.
    void
    purgeExpired()
    Permanently removes every trashed file whose retention period has elapsed.
    void
    restore(String rootPath, String userId, String entryId)
    Restores a trashed file back to its original location, recreating parent folders if needed.

  • Field Details

    • TRASH_DIR

      public static final String TRASH_DIR
      Name of the per-user trash directory, relative to the user's root.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • TrashService

      public TrashService()

  • Method Details

    • moveToTrash

      public void moveToTrash(String rootPath, String userId, String relativeFilePath) throws IOException
      Moves a file into the user's trash instead of deleting it permanently.
      Parameters:
      rootPath - the root directory of the user, used as a security boundary
      userId - the id of the owning user
      relativeFilePath - the path, relative to the root, of the file to trash
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be moved
      ResourceNotFoundException - if the file does not exist or is not a regular file

    • listTrash

      public List<TrashEntryDto> listTrash(String userId)
      Lists the current contents of a user's trash.
      Parameters:
      userId - the id of the owning user
      Returns:
      the trashed entries belonging to the user

    • restore

      public void restore(String rootPath, String userId, String entryId) throws IOException
      Restores a trashed file back to its original location, recreating parent folders if needed.
      Parameters:
      rootPath - the root directory of the user, used as a security boundary
      userId - the id of the owning user
      entryId - the id of the trash entry to restore
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be moved back
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no matching trash entry exists for the user

    • purge

      public void purge(String rootPath, String userId, String entryId) throws IOException
      Permanently removes a single file from the user's trash.
      Parameters:
      rootPath - the root directory of the user, used as a security boundary
      userId - the id of the owning user
      entryId - the id of the trash entry to remove
      Throws:
      IOException - if the file cannot be deleted
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no matching trash entry exists for the user

    • purgeExpired

      @Scheduled(cron="${cloudpage.trash.cleanup-cron:0 0 3 * * *}") public void purgeExpired()
      Permanently removes every trashed file whose retention period has elapsed. Runs on a schedule (daily by default).
